g.text: The Alpha 1 aims to capture the inverse relationship between the rank of the opening prices and the rank of the trading volume over the past 10 days…The Alpha 2 attempts to exploit the relationship between price volatility and trading volume to generate predictive insights for stock returns…

In the crossover alpha, I exchanged elements from the original alphas to create a new combination… This new alpha aims to capture the relationship between the rank of trading volume and the standard deviation of opening prices, as well as their correlation with high prices…Therefore the Crossover Alpha is: ((-1 * rank(stddev(open, 10))) * correlation(rank(volume), high, 10))

In the mutate alpha, I introduced a mutation by adding the time-series minimum operator (ts_min) to the crossover alpha... This introduces a new computational element into the alpha, aiming to capture the minimum value of the expression over the past 15 days…Therefore the Mutation Alpha is: ts_min((-1 * rank(stddev(open, 10))) * correlation(rank(volume), high, 10), 15)
Fitness value: ICError Detected RegenerateNo error

# Calculate the standard deviation of opening prices over a 10-day perioddf['stddev_open_10'] = df['open'].rolling(window=10).std()# Rank the standard deviation and multiply by -1df['rank_stddev_open_10'] = df['stddev_open_10'].rank(axis=1, pct=True) * -1df['rank_volume'] = df['volume'].rank(axis=1, pct=True)df['correlation_rank_volume_high_10'] = df['rank_volume'].rolling(window=10).corr(df['high'])# Calculate the time-series min over the past 15 days of the product of the previous factorsdf['alpha'] = df['rank_stddev_open_10'] *df['correlation_rank_volume_high_10']df['alpha'] = df['alpha'].rolling(window=15).min()

{ Generated Alpha 1:{“alpha”: ts_min((-1 * rank(stddev(open, 10))) * correlation(rank(volume),high, 10), 15),“code”: “Calculate the …”, “fitness_value”: 0.0383}}……Generated Alpha G*N: {“alpha”: ……,“code”: ……,“fitness_value”: ……}}
